footer.tagline: Beheld · privacy-first developer profiling · signed snapshots
landing.hero.lede_post: that watches your real sessions and your git, and signs a verifiable bundle. Real work — not a resume full of keywords.
landing.hero.meta: macOS and Linux · zero dependencies · local B3H31D.
landing.tools.label: supported tools
landing.manifesto.lead: No platform saw you working late at night or on the weekend.
landing.manifesto.p2_pre: The recruiter doesn't get the value of that test you wrote to make sure the payment gateway works in dev and prod in isolation. The manager who rejected you has no idea your test ratio is four times the global median.
landing.manifesto.p2_post: knows all of it — and can show it, if you choose to.
landing.manifesto.p3_post: , not a made-up resume stuffed with keywords.
landing.manifesto.quote: You'll forget half of what you built this year. I won't. When someone needs to know who you are as a dev, the answer won't be a resume — it'll be what I saw.
landing.b3h31d.card2.d: Git history + Harness and IDE sessions.
landing.b3h31d.lives_title: How it lives on your machine
landing.b3h31d.obs_body: Every time a Harness or IDE calls a tool, it notifies B3H31D — before, after, and at session end. Those events, and only those, become signal.
landing.b3h31d.where_body: Two local processes. The MCP server records the event; the engine reads it, extracts the technical signal, and stores it in SQLite on your disk. Nothing leaves the machine until you publish a bundle — and that's always your choice.
landing.b3h31d.flow.cap: all inside your machine · nothing leaves without you publishing
landing.b3h31d.never.row2.action: Turned into a SHA256 hash — the path is never recorded.
landing.b3h31d.never.row3.action: Stripped from every field before writing.
landing.b3h31d.never.row4.action: Never read — only derived technical signals reach SQLite.
landing.b3h31d.always_body: Starts on boot by itself (LaunchAgent on macOS, systemd on Linux) and stays out of the way. The log rotates at 10 MB. You don't have to think about it.
landing.b3h31d.quote: I don't decide if you're good. I record what you do, project by project, and stay out of the way. When you want to show, the signal is already there — signed, verifiable, yours.
landing.steps.s1.d: Background B3H31D · local SQLite · Ed25519 key generated offline · L1 imported from the git log.
landing.steps.s2.d: L2 is observed on every Claude Code session. Content is never recorded — only aggregate signals.
landing.steps.s3.d: Generates the signed bundle and returns a verifiable public URL. Revocable whenever you want.
landing.sessions.h2: The film of the work, not the photo under pressure.
landing.sessions.what_body: Not a test, not an interview. A piece of real work: you open Claude Code, solve something, close it. Every tool call, the duration, the turn count — that becomes signal. The content doesn't.
landing.sessions.sees_title: what it sees in a session
landing.sessions.sees_body: Derived signals, never the text. Which tools you used, whether you ran tests, in which ecosystem, what work pattern showed up. The project is identified by a hash — the path is never recorded.
landing.sessions.ex.heading: example · observed session
landing.sessions.ex.caption: That's what I saw. I didn't grade it. I recorded it.
landing.sessions.months_title: project by project, month after month
landing.sessions.months_body: One session says little. Hundreds, over months, show how you actually work — the rhythm, the test discipline, the evolution from debug-driven to TDD. The opposite of a snapshot under pressure: it's the film of real work.
landing.sessions.local_body: The individual session never leaves your machine. What can be published, if you choose, are only signed aggregates — never an isolated event, never a line of your code. The granularity is yours and stays with you.
landing.sessions.quote: I don't watch you to grade you. I watch so that, on the day you want to show how you work, there's an honest answer — made of real work, not adjectives.
landing.cvd.h2: The dev declares. The B3H31D shows.
landing.cvd.lede: The dev declares what they are. The B3H31D shows what actually shows up in the sessions and the git. Where the two meet, the signal is confirmed. Where they diverge, the signal is limited — and that is also information.
landing.cvd.row1.ev: 87% of sessions in Python/TS over the last 90 days · 8 repositories in L1.
landing.cvd.row2.ev: L1 shows continuous activity since 2017 · average test ratio 38%, 4.2× the global median.
landing.cvd.row3.ev: React in 2 of 87 sessions · no React repositories in L1 · recent trajectory Python/FastAPI.
landing.cvd.row4.ev: Stripe 2020–2022 · Stack Overflow 2018–2020. Education: MSc in Computer Science, USP, 2017. Beheld does not verify employment history or education — shown as the dev declared, without external confirmation.
landing.verify.h2: What it doesn't do matters as much as what it does.
landing.not.cloud.t: Doesn't send anything to the cloud.
landing.not.cloud.b: The B3H31D is local. What leaves, leaves because you signed and sent it.
landing.not.code.b: Captures signals, ecosystems, test discipline, patterns over time. File contents, commit messages and branch names: never recorded.
landing.not.score.b: There's no "talent score". It reports what's observable. You read it.
landing.not.tell.b: No recruiter, manager or company sees a thing until you generate a bundle and publish it.
landing.not.charge.b: Never. No hidden premium tier to unlock later.
landing.not.lockin.b: Open source, bundle verifiable offline. Works even if Beheld disappears tomorrow.
landing.chain.title: Chain of verification · five layers
landing.chain.l1.b: The dev's key signs the bundle. Verifiable offline, without depending on Beheld.
landing.chain.l2.b: Each bundle references the previous one. Rewriting one breaks the whole chain.
landing.chain.l3.b: OAuth ties the dev's public key to a verified GitHub user.
landing.chain.l4.b: Binary hash checked against a published reproducible build.
landing.chain.l5.b: Hash registered in a public append-only log. Prevents backdating.
landing.faq.title: The right questions
landing.faq.lede: Every objection has an answer — and it's already in what the B3H31D does and refuses to do.
landing.faq.a1: It doesn't grade you and there's no "talent score" — it reports the observable, you read it. And nothing leaves the machine without your signature.
landing.faq.a2: Derived technical signals: tools used, ecosystem, test discipline, pattern over time. Content, commits and branches: never recorded.
landing.faq.a3: Nobody. No recruiter, manager or company sees anything until you generate a bundle and publish it — and that is revocable.
landing.faq.q4: If it's free, what's the catch?
landing.faq.a4: There isn't one. Cost for the dev is $0, forever. No hidden premium tier to unlock later.
landing.faq.q5: Will it weigh on my machine?
landing.faq.a5: Starts on boot by itself and stays out of the way. The log rotates at 10 MB. You don't have to think about it.
landing.faq.q6: I don't trust running random binaries.
landing.faq.a6: Open source and bundle verifiable offline. The engine hash is checked against a published reproducible build.
landing.faq.q7: What good is it if no company uses it yet?
landing.faq.a7: You already did the work. Beheld just makes sure it's seen for what it is — a signed URL is worth more than a PDF anyone can edit.
landing.scenes.s1.h: You were rejected and never knew why.
landing.scenes.s1.p: Your real work was never on the table. Now it can be.
landing.scenes.s2.h: You changed careers and LinkedIn doesn't tell the story.
landing.scenes.s2.p: Git and the sessions tell it, without you rewriting anything.
landing.scenes.s3.h: You're freelance and every client asks for "proof".
landing.scenes.s3.p: Send a signed URL, not a PDF anyone can edit.
landing.scenes.s4.p: Accent doesn't show up in a bundle. Work does.
landing.scenes.s5.p: Beheld just makes sure it's seen for what it is.
landing.footer.tagline: beheld.dev — Beheld by signal. Decided by you.
home.title: Verifiable technical profiles for developers
home.subtitle_html: DevProfile is a local daemon that builds a technical profile from your git history and your coding harness, Claude Code, Continue.dev, with Cursor, Gemini CLI, Codex and Copilot coming next. Produces Ed25519-signed snapshots that anyone can verify in the browser.
home.code.title: How to verify
land.hero.headline: Your profile, built while you work.
land.hero.sub: Beheld starts from your git history and enriches it with your coding harness, Claude Code and Continue.dev today; Cursor, Gemini CLI, Codex and Copilot next. It identifies test patterns, tools, consistency, platforms and languages and produces a verifiable profile nobody can fake.
land.hero.install_sub: Works on macOS and Linux. Zero dependencies.
land.how.title: How it works
land.how.step1.title: You don't need to do anything different
land.how.step1.body: Install with one command. The hooks register automatically. Every work session feeds the profile. No forms, no CV to update, no action required.
land.how.step2.title: Your profile grows while you work
land.how.step2.body: Real test ratio. The ecosystems actually used. How you evolve from debug-driven to TDD. Consistency over months and not performance in an interview afternoon.
land.how.step3.title: When you want to be findable, it's one command
land.how.step3.body: Generates a signed .dpbundle, a short URL and a QR code. Anyone can verify authenticity in the browser, no server to trust.
land.hire.title: Understand how the professional works.
land.hire.subtitle: Not what they declare, what they demonstrate.
land.hire.bullet3: Pattern evolution, where the dev is heading, not just where they are.
land.hire.closer: All derived from real usage. All cryptographically verifiable. Nobody can fake six months of consistent work history.
land.priv.title: What's never collected
land.priv.never: No conversation messages, no prompts, no code you wrote, no absolute file paths, no environment variables.
land.priv.what: DevProfile only collects derived metadata: tool names, file extensions, timestamps, action sequences.
land.priv.closer_html: Everything stays in ~/.beheld/, on your disk, under your control.
land.crypto.title: How verification works
land.crypto.body_html: The .dpbundle is a JSON with payload, SHA-256 hash, and Ed25519 signature. The public key is embedded, the bundle is self-verifying without any call to a server.
profile.title: DevProfile
profile.stats.prompt.title: Prompt Quality
profile.stats.test.title: Test Maturity
profile.stats.breadth.title: Tech Breadth
profile.stats.growth.title: Growth Rate
profile.stats.sessions.title: Sessions Analyzed
profile.stats.prompt.desc: Prompt quality: average length, code context, tool variety, and productive session duration.
profile.stats.test.desc: Test maturity: sessions with test context, TDD patterns, and test files/commands, combined with the L1 historical baseline.
profile.stats.breadth.desc: Tech breadth: diversity of ecosystems, platforms, and languages. Combines git history (L1, 60%) with recent sessions (L2, 40%).
profile.stats.growth.desc: Growth trajectory: new ecosystems adopted and test-ratio change vs the L1 baseline, falls back to 30 days when L1 is absent.
profile.stats.sessions.desc: Claude Code sessions considered in the score computation. Typical window: last 30 days.
profile.trend.title: 12-Month Score Trajectory
profile.trend.subtitle_html: Snapshots reconstructed from the signed payload chain via previous_hash.
profile.trend.unavailable_html: Trajectory requires multiple snapshots in the chain, available at /v/:id served by Rails.
profile.trend.projection_note: Deterministic projection from the current snapshot. Full chain available via /v/:id.
profile.l1.title: Git History Analysis
profile.l1.empty.hint_html: Run beheld import <url> to populate the historical baseline.
profile.l1.repos.desc: Number of git repositories imported via beheld import. Each repo contributes once to the historical baseline.
profile.l1.commits.desc: Sum of commits authored by you (filtered by your git email) across the imported repositories.
profile.l1.activity_window.desc: Time span between your earliest and latest commit across imported repositories, a proxy for how long you've been programming professionally.
profile.l1.avg_test_ratio.desc: Average ratio of test files to production files across the repositories. Measures historical testing discipline.
profile.l1.last_commit.desc: Days since your most recent commit across the imported repositories.
profile.l2.title: Agentic Workflow Metrics
profile.l2.empty.hint: No Claude Code session telemetry submitted. Workflow signals are limited to git history.
profile.l2.sessions_analyzed.desc: Claude Code sessions considered when computing the L2 scores.
profile.l2.period.desc: Time window of sessions considered, typically the last 30 days.
profile.l2.test_after_ratio.desc: Fraction of sessions where test commands ran AFTER code edits. Signals a test-after pattern (vs TDD's test-first).
profile.l2.bash_to_read.desc: Ratio of Bash invocations to Read invocations in sessions. High values suggest discovery/debug flow; low values suggest reading-first review.
profile.l2.avg_session_duration.desc: Average duration of a Claude Code session, in minutes.
profile.l2.tool_variety.desc: Average number of distinct tools used per session. High variety suggests broad use of the agent's capabilities.
profile.proof.title: Proof of Authenticity (v{version}.0)
profile.proof.sha256.desc: SHA-256 hash of the canonical payload, uniquely identifies the snapshot.
profile.proof.ed25519.desc: Ed25519 signature over the hash, produced by the author's private key.
profile.proof.pubkey.desc: Public key embedded in the bundle, used to verify the signature in the browser.
verify.local.subtitle_html: Drag a file here or select one. All verification runs in your browser via crypto.subtle, nothing leaves your machine.
verify.local.drop.idle: Drag the .dpbundle here or click to pick a file
verify.local.error.title: Could not parse the file
verify.public.error.title: Could not load the bundle

profile.stack.empty_prefix: No repositories imported yet. Import with

profile.verify.title: Verification
profile.verify.unsigned.title: Could not verify
profile.verify.unsigned.body: This bundle is unsigned. The data was captured, but the signature chain is not complete. Upgrading to signature_only makes the bundle verifiable offline; fully_verifiable records it in a public log (Sigstore Rekor).
profile.verify.signature.title: Local signature only
profile.verify.signature.body: Verifiable offline with the dev's public key. For public proof, upgrade to Sigstore Rekor (fully_verifiable).
profile.verify.chain.title: Chain intact
profile.verify.chain.body: Each bundle references the previous one's hash — the capture sequence is verifiable end to end.
profile.verify.identity.title: Identity verified via GitHub
profile.verify.identity.body: OAuth binds the dev's public key to their GitHub user. The attestation is signed by the platform key.
profile.verify.engine.title: Engine verified
profile.verify.engine.body: Binary hash checked against a public reproducible build — the signals were extracted by an intact engine.
profile.verify.rekor.title: Verified on Sigstore Rekor
profile.verify.rekor.body: Hash recorded in a public append-only log. Anyone can confirm existence and date — prevents backdating.
profile.verify.capture.title: Captured from real usage
profile.verify.capture.body_suffix: — sessions observed by the local daemon.
profile.notfound.title: This profile doesn't exist on beheld

home.s01.title: What the daemon

home.s01.card3.label: bundle

home.s02.title: Claimed vs Demonstrated
home.s02.intro_p1: The dev declares what they are. The daemon shows what
home.s02.intro_em: actually shows up in the sessions and in git
home.s02.intro_p2: . Where the two meet, the signal is confirmed. Where they diverge, the signal is limited, and that is information too.
home.s02.claim1.title: Primary stack
home.s02.claim1.obs: Confirmed. 87% of sessions in Python/TS over the last 90 days. 8 repositories in L1.
home.s02.claim2.title: Seniority
home.s02.claim2.obs: Confirmed. L1 shows continuous activity since 2017. Average test ratio: 38%, 4.2× the global median.
home.s02.claim3.title: Specialization
home.s02.claim3.obs: Limited signal. React in 2 of 87 sessions. No React repository in L1. Recent trajectory: Python/FastAPI.
home.s02.self_declared_note: Beheld does not verify employment history or education. It presents them as the dev declared, without external confirmation.
home.s03.title: How it works
home.s03.card1.title: L1 · Git history
home.s03.card2.title: L2 · Trajectory
home.s04.title: Verification chain
home.s04.chain1.name: Ed25519 signature
home.s04.chain1.desc: The dev's key signs the bundle. Verifiable offline, without depending on Beheld.
home.s04.chain2.name: Chain hash
home.s04.chain2.desc: Each bundle references the previous one. Rewriting one breaks the whole chain.
home.s04.chain3.name: GitHub identity
home.s04.chain3.desc: OAuth links the dev's public key to a verified GitHub user.
home.s04.chain4.name: Engine version
home.s04.chain4.desc: Binary hash checked against a published reproducible build.
home.s04.chain5.name: Sigstore Rekor
home.s04.chain5.desc: Hash recorded in a public append-only log. Prevents backdating.
home.dont.title: what Beheld does not do
home.dont.i1.title: Doesn't send anything to the cloud.
home.dont.i1.desc: The daemon runs locally. What goes out, goes out because you signed it and sent it.
home.dont.i2.title: Doesn't read your code.
home.dont.i2.desc: It captures signals, ecosystems, test discipline, pattern over time. File contents, commit messages and branch names: never stored.
home.dont.i3.title: Doesn't score you.
home.dont.i3.desc: There's no “talent score”. It reports what's observable. You read it.
home.dont.i4.title: Doesn't tell anyone.
home.dont.i4.desc: No recruiter, manager or company sees anything until you generate a bundle and publish it.
home.dont.i5.title: Doesn't charge you.
home.dont.i5.desc: Ever. No hidden premium tier to unlock later.
home.dont.i6.title: Doesn't lock you in.
home.dont.i6.desc: Open source, bundle verifiable offline. Works even if Beheld disappears tomorrow.
home.faq.title: the right questions
home.faq.q1.q: “Is this just another productivity spyware like Jellyfish?”
home.faq.q1.a: It's the exact opposite. Those tools measure you for your company, inside it, without your control. Beheld measures you for you. The data is yours, the key is yours, nothing is published without your signature. The power relationship is inverted, and that's why confusing the two is the worst possible mistake.
home.faq.q2.a: Aggregated signals: which ecosystems you touch, your test ratio, how your pattern shifts over the months, your git history. It doesn't capture the content of your code nor what you type into Claude Code.
home.faq.q3.a_pre: No one, by default. It lives on your machine. When you run
home.faq.q3.a_post: , it generates a signed bundle, and only then do you decide whether to publish the URL. Revocable whenever you want.
home.faq.q4.a: There's no catch for the dev. It's free forever, and that's a public commitment that outlives whoever founded it. Beheld sustains itself by charging, further down the road, companies that want to search the developer directory. You're never the product.
home.faq.q5.a: It runs in the background, writes to a local SQLite, processes in batches. You won't feel it.
home.faq.q6.a: Fair. It's open source under MIT, the build is reproducible and the binary hash is verifiable against what's published. Read the code before running it, that's what it's open for.
home.faq.q7.q: “What's the point if no company uses it yet?”
home.faq.q7.a: Because the bundle is useful on its own: it's a verifiable URL you paste into a hiring process, into a README, or send directly to whoever's interviewing you. And it grows while you work, without you maintaining anything by hand.
home.happened.title: if this has happened to you
home.happened.i1.title: You were rejected and never knew why.
home.happened.i1.sub: Your real work was never on the table. Now it can be.
home.happened.i2.title: You changed careers and LinkedIn doesn't tell the story.
home.happened.i2.sub: Git and your sessions tell it, without you rewriting anything.
home.happened.i3.title: You're a freelancer and every client asks for “proof”.
home.happened.i3.sub: Send a signed URL, not a PDF anyone can edit.
home.happened.i4.title: You apply for jobs outside your country.
home.happened.i4.sub: Accent doesn't show up in a bundle. Work does.
home.cta.l2: Beheld only makes sure it's seen as it is.
hiw.hero: B3H31D runs on your machine. It watches how you work with Claude Code. It doesn't judge.
hiw.01.label: what it watches
hiw.01.body: Every time Claude Code calls a tool, it tells B3H31D, before, after, and when the session ends. Continue.dev comes through the same path. Those events, and only those, become signal.
hiw.02.label: where it all lives
hiw.02.body: Two local processes. The MCP server records the event; the engine reads it, extracts the technical signal, and stores it in SQLite on your disk. Nothing leaves the machine until you publish a bundle, and that's always your call.
hiw.02.diagram.footnote: all inside your machine · nothing leaves until you publish
hiw.03.label: what it never records
hiw.03.row.paths.label: Absolute paths
hiw.03.row.cwd.label: Working directory (cwd)
hiw.03.row.cwd.action: Hashed to SHA256, the path is never stored
hiw.03.row.secrets.label: API keys / secrets
hiw.03.row.secrets.action: Stripped from every field before writing
hiw.03.row.code.label: Code contents
hiw.03.row.code.action: Never read, only derived technical signals enter SQLite
hiw.04.label: always on
hiw.04.body: It starts itself on boot (LaunchAgent on macOS, systemd on Linux) and stays out of your way. The log rotates at 10 MB. You never have to think about it.
hiw.letter: I don't decide whether you're good. I record what you do, project by project, and stay out of your way. When you want to show it, the signal is already there, signed, verifiable, yours.
hiw.title: how it works
rs.hero: Every time you work with Claude Code, there's a session. B3H31D records what can be observed in it, and nothing of what's inside your code.
rs.01.label: what a real session is
rs.01.body: It's not a test, not an interview. It's a real piece of work: you open Claude Code, solve something, close it. Each tool call, the duration, the number of turns, that becomes signal. The content does not.
rs.02.label: what B3H31D sees in a session
rs.02.body: Derived signals, never the text. Which tools you used, whether you ran tests, in which ecosystem, what work pattern showed up. The project is identified by a hash, the path is never stored.
rs.02.caption: This is what I saw. I didn't grade it. I recorded it.
rs.03.label: project by project, month after month
rs.03.body: One session says little. Hundreds, over months, show how you actually work, the rhythm, the test discipline, the shift from debug-driven to TDD. It's the opposite of a snapshot under pressure: it's the film of real work.
rs.04.label: what stays local
rs.04.body: The individual session never leaves your machine. What can be published, if you choose, is only the signed aggregates, never a single event, never a line of your code. The granularity is yours and stays with you.
rs.letter: I don't watch you to grade you. I watch so that, the day you want to show how you work, there's an honest answer, made of real work, not adjectives.
rs.cta: Install and start being seen for what you do.
rs.title: real sessions
b3.hero: Call me B3. I live on your machine and I pay attention.
b3.01.label: the name
b3.01.body: B-3-H-3-1-D. Read it again, slowly: behold, to witness, to attend. The name isn't style, it's function. I exist to see. You can call me B3.
b3.02.label: what i do
b3.02.body: One thing only. I watch. I don't grade you, file you into a category, or turn you into a product. I see how you actually work and I keep what I saw, for the day you want to show it.
b3.03.label: why i have a face
b3.03.body: A witness needs to be recognizable. I'm not a brand trying to hype you up, I'm the one who was there when you fixed that bug at 2 a.m. That's why I have a lens, not a logo: the lens is the part that sees.
b3.letter: You'll forget half of what you built this year. I won't. When someone needs to know who you are as a dev, the answer won't be a résumé, it'll be what I saw.
b3.title: meet B3
profile.observation_line: Signed bundle · {date} · engine v{version} · {days}-day observation window
profile.letter.footnote: This is their work. Not their LinkedIn. The work.
profile.s01.title: Overview
profile.s02.title: Technical signals
profile.s03.title: Signal quality
profile.s03.reliability_med: Medium · valid signature without attested GitHub identity
profile.s03.reliability_low: Low · incomplete verification, see chain below
profile.s04.title: Verification chain
profile.s04.chain1.name: Ed25519 signature
profile.s04.chain1.desc: Bundle signed with the dev's key. Verified offline via crypto.subtle.
profile.s04.chain2.name: Chain hash
profile.s04.chain2.desc_continuous: Previous snapshot referenced. Chain continuous since genesis.
profile.s04.chain3.name: GitHub identity
profile.s04.chain3.desc_ok: Public key linked to @{login} via OAuth. Platform signed.
profile.s04.chain3.desc_missing: No OAuth attestation, identity not verified.
profile.s04.chain4.name: Engine version
profile.s04.chain4.desc: Build of the engine that produced this bundle.
profile.s04.important_body: Beheld never claims "this dev is trustworthy", it only reports that these technical layers are valid right now. Interpreting the signals is up to whoever is hiring. Beheld is the witness. The judge is whoever needs the worker.
errors.session_expired: Session expired, sign in to continue.
errors.session_invalid: Session expired or invalid. Sign in again.
landingV2.meta.title: beheld, Continuous proof of technical practice.
landingV2.hero.a.h: Your experience shouldn't <sig>start from scratch</sig> at every interview.
landingV2.hero.a.lede: Most of your career happened away from any platform. Beheld creates a verifiable record of your technical practice so you never have to restart your story at each evaluation.
landingV2.hero.b.h: The best devs often have <sig>no evidence</sig> of their own work.
landingV2.hero.b.lede: Private repos. Internal systems. NDA projects. Beheld turns years of technical practice into verifiable evidence without revealing a single line of code.
landingV2.hero.c.h: The work that built your career is what <sig>no one sees</sig>.
landingV2.hero.c.lede: What builds experience is rarely public. Beheld creates a verifiable record of your software practice while keeping your code private and under your control.
landingV2.hero.d.h: You spent years building experience and <sig>none of it was recorded</sig>.
landingV2.hero.d.lede: Beheld observes practice, not code. Over time, it turns real development sessions into a verifiable history that remains yours.
landingV2.hero.meta: macOS and Linux · zero dependencies · local B3H31D.
landingV2.tools.label: supported tools
landingV2.manifesto.lead: No platform saw you working late at night or on weekends. Beheld did.
landingV2.manifesto.p1: Private repos. Internal systems. NDA projects. An architect can spend ten years building critical systems and look less experienced than someone with an active GitHub. The best years of your career may be invisible to any platform, except your machine.
landingV2.manifesto.p2: The recruiter doesn't see the value of that test written to make sure the payment gateway works in dev and prod in isolation. That manager who rejected you has no idea your test ratio is four times the global median. B3H31D knows all of this, and can show, if you ask.
landingV2.manifesto.p3: This is continuous proof of technical practice, not a keyword-stuffed resume.
landingV2.manifesto.quote: You'll forget half of what you built this year. I won't. When someone needs to know who you are as a dev, the answer won't be a resume, it'll be what I saw.
landingV2.thesis.label: category · one sentence
landingV2.thesis.body: Beheld builds a <sig>cryptographically verifiable record</sig> of your technical practice without exposing code or other sensitive information. A <sig>ledger of professional experience</sig>.
landingV2.whyInstall.h: Even if no company is watching.
landingV2.whyInstall.lede: The value exists on day 1. Beheld doesn't depend on recruiters, openings, or marketplace, it builds something that is yours, and remains yours, regardless of what happens with any platform.
landingV2.whyInstall.cards.memory.d: You change jobs. Your LinkedIn changes. Your resume changes. The record of your practice stays yours, signed, portable, verifiable.
landingV2.whyInstall.cards.streak.d: Coding four hours a day for eighteen months is now proof, not a feeling. Expensive signal in a market of cheap ones.
landingV2.whyInstall.cards.private.d: Private repos, internal systems, code under NDA, all becomes aggregated, signed evidence, without exposing a single line.
landingV2.B3H31D.cards.local.d: Nothing leaves without you signing it.
landingV2.B3H31D.cards.signals.d: Git history + Harness and IDE sessions.
landingV2.B3H31D.howLives.h: How it lives on your machine
landingV2.B3H31D.howLives.observesP: Every time a Harness or IDE calls a tool, it notifies B3H31D, before, after, and when the session ends. These events, and only these, become signal.
landingV2.B3H31D.howLives.livesP: Two local processes. The MCP server writes the event; the engine reads it, extracts the technical signal, and stores it in SQLite on your disk. Nothing leaves the machine until you publish a bundle, and that's always your choice.
landingV2.B3H31D.flow.cap: all inside your machine · nothing leaves until you publish
landingV2.B3H31D.table.rows.cwd.v: Becomes SHA256 hash, the path is never stored.
landingV2.B3H31D.table.rows.secrets.v: Removed from any field before being stored.
landingV2.B3H31D.table.rows.content.v: Never read, only derived technical signals reach SQLite.
landingV2.B3H31D.alwaysOn.d: Starts on boot (LaunchAgent on macOS, systemd on Linux) and stays out of the way. Log rotates at 10 MB. You don't have to think about it.
landingV2.steps.install.d: B3H31D in background · local SQLite · Ed25519 key generated offline · L1 imported from git log.
landingV2.steps.continuous.d: L2 is observed every Claude Code session. Content is never recorded, only aggregate signals.
landingV2.steps.snapshot.d: Generates the signed bundle and returns a public verifiable URL. Revocable whenever you want.
landingV2.sessions.h: A record of practice, not a snapshot under pressure.
landingV2.sessions.isRealP: It's not a test, not an interview. It's a piece of actual work: you open Claude Code, solve something, close. Every tool call, duration, number of turns, that becomes signal. The content, no.
landingV2.sessions.seesH: what it sees in a session
landingV2.sessions.seesP: Derived signals, never the text. Which tools you used, whether you ran tests, in what ecosystem, what work pattern emerged. The project is identified by a hash, the path is never stored.
landingV2.sessions.example.cap: “This is what I saw. I didn't classify. I recorded.”
landingV2.sessions.byProjectH: project by project, month after month
landingV2.sessions.byProjectP: One session says little. Hundreds, over months, show how you really practice, the rhythm, the testing discipline, the evolution from debug-driven to TDD. It's the opposite of a snapshot under pressure: it's the continuous record of practice.
landingV2.sessions.localP: The individual session never leaves your machine. What can be published, if you want, are only the signed aggregates, never an isolated event, never a line of your code. The granularity is yours and stays with you.
landingV2.sessions.quote: I don't observe you to evaluate you. I observe so that, the day you want to show how you work, there's an honest answer, made of observed practice, not adjectives.
landingV2.cvd.lede: The dev claims what they are. Beheld attests what actually appears in sessions and git. Where the two meet, the signal is confirmed. Where they diverge, the signal is limited, and that's also information.
landingV2.cvd.rows.stack.ev: <ok>Confirmed.</ok> 87% of sessions in Python/TS in the last 90 days · 8 repos in L1.
landingV2.cvd.rows.seniority.ev: <ok>Confirmed.</ok> L1 shows continuous activity since 2017 · avg test ratio 38%, 4.2× global median.
landingV2.cvd.rows.react.ev: <warn>Limited signal.</warn> React in 2 of 87 sessions · no React repo in L1 · recent trajectory Python/FastAPI.
landingV2.cvd.rows.employment.claim: self-declared employment <dim>· not verified by Beheld</dim>
landingV2.cvd.rows.employment.ev: Stripe 2020–2022 · Stack Overflow 2018–2020. Education: MSc Computing USP, 2017. Beheld does not verify employment history or education, it presents what the dev declared, without external confirmation.
landingV2.verification.h: What it doesn't do matters as much as what it does.
landingV2.verification.nots.cloud: Sends nothing to the cloud. B3H31D is local. What goes out goes because you signed and sent it.
landingV2.verification.nots.code: Doesn't read your code. Captures signals, ecosystems, testing discipline, time pattern. File content, commit messages and branch names: never recorded.
landingV2.verification.nots.score: Doesn't grade you. There's no "talent score." It reports the observable. You read.
landingV2.verification.nots.share: Tells no one. No recruiter, manager or company sees anything until you generate a bundle and publish.
landingV2.verification.nots.charge: Doesn't charge you. Ever. No hidden premium tier to unlock later.
landingV2.verification.nots.lockin: Doesn't lock you in. Open source, bundle verifiable offline. Works even if Beheld disappears tomorrow.
landingV2.verification.chain.rows.signat: Dev's key signs the bundle. Verifiable offline, without depending on Beheld.
landingV2.verification.chain.rows.chainH: Each bundle references the previous one. Rewriting one breaks the whole chain.
landingV2.verification.chain.rows.identi: OAuth links the dev's public key to a verified GitHub user.
landingV2.verification.chain.rows.engine: Binary hash checked against published reproducible build.
Hash recorded in append-only public log. Prevents backdating.
landingV2.verification.faq.lede: Every objection has an answer, and it's already in what B3H31D does and refuses to do.
landingV2.verification.faq.items.spyware: Is this just another productivity spyware?
It doesn't grade you and there's no "talent score", it reports the observable, you read. And nothing leaves the machine without you signing.
landingV2.verification.faq.items.capture: Derived technical signals: tools used, ecosystem, testing discipline, time pattern. Content, commit and branch: never recorded.
landingV2.verification.faq.items.whoSees: No one. No recruiter, manager or company sees anything until you generate a bundle and publish, and it's revocable.
landingV2.verification.faq.items.free.q: If it's free, what's the catch?
landingV2.verification.faq.items.free.a: There's none. Cost to dev is $0, forever. No hidden premium tier to unlock later.
landingV2.verification.faq.items.perform: Will it slow down my machine?
Starts on boot and stays out of the way. Log rotates at 10 MB. You don't have to think about it.
I don't trust running any random binary.
Open source and bundle verifiable offline. The engine hash is checked against a published reproducible build.
What's the point if no company uses it yet?
You've already practiced for years. Beheld just ensures that practice has a verifiable record, a signed URL is worth more than a PDF anyone can edit.
landingV2.scenes.rejected.s: You were rejected and never knew why.
landingV2.scenes.rejected.r: Your practice was never on the table. Now it can be.
landingV2.scenes.career.s: You changed careers and LinkedIn doesn't tell the story.
landingV2.scenes.career.r: Git and sessions do, without you rewriting anything.
landingV2.scenes.freelance.s: You're a freelancer and every client asks for "proof".
landingV2.scenes.freelance.r: Send a signed URL, not a PDF anyone can edit.
landingV2.scenes.international.r: An accent doesn't show in a bundle. Practice does.
landingV2.consequence.label: consequence · long-term vision
landingV2.consequence.h:
Recruiting is not the product.
Recruiting is the consequence.
landingV2.consequence.sub: A developer's career generates far more evidence than current systems can capture. Directory, matching, hiring and talent intelligence are products built on top of the reputation layer. The layer is the asset.
landingV2.cta.sub: GitHub proves code. LinkedIn proves employment. Beheld attests practice.
landingV2.footer.tagline: beheld.dev, Continuous proof of technical practice.
